mission.title: Install and Configure Opengemini on Kubernetes
mission.description: openGemini is a cloud-native distributed time-series database designed for high concurrency, performance, and scalability. Installing it allows you to efficiently store and analyze massive telemetry data in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the openGemini Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official openGemini Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add opengemini https://charts.opengemini.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install openGemini using Helm
steps.description:
Install openGemini in a new namespace called 'opengemini' with the following command:
```bash
helm install opengemini opengemini/opengemini --namespace opengemini --create-namespace --version v1.5.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the openGemini pods are running with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace opengemini
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the openGemini deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your default editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment opengemini --namespace opengemini
```
Add the following under the container spec:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access openGemini
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access openGemini from your local machine with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/opengemini 8080:80 --namespace opengemini
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have openGemini running in the 'opengemini' namespace with its pods in the 'Running' state. You should be able to access it via port 8080 on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add opengemini https://charts.opengemini.org
helm repo update
helm install opengemini opengemini/opengemini --namespace opengemini --create-namespace --version v1.5.2

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy openGemini.
